title: salix × algista
intro: Salix × algista, also known as the crack willow, is a hybrid plant derived from the cross between Salix alba and Salix fragilis, two species of willow trees. The bark and leaves of the crack willow contain salicylic acid, a compound with anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, which has been used in traditional medicine for various ailments. The medicinal properties of Salix × algista have been associated with reducing fever, relieving headaches and joint pain, and aiding in the treatment of digestive issues, such as diarrhea and indigestion. The plant's herbal preparations, often in the form of tea or tincture, have been used to support cardiovascular health, alleviate menstrual cramps, and promote relaxation due to its sedative effects.
